report_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act's Strict Reporting Obligations for Connected Products to Begin in September 2026

identities.name: European Union
identities.description: A political and economic union that has established the Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) to enforce cybersecurity requirements for products with digital elements sold within its market.

identities.name: ENISA
identities.description: The European Union Agency for Cybersecurity, tasked with receiving mandatory 24-hour reports of actively exploited vulnerabilities from manufacturers under the Cyber Resilience Act.

identities.name: Computer Security Incident Response Team
identities.description: National CSIRTs are designated to receive vulnerability reports from manufacturers under the Cyber Resilience Act, alongside ENISA.

identities.name: Manufacturers, importers, and distributors
identities.description: The class of organizations legally mandated by the EU Cyber Resilience Act to report actively exploited vulnerabilities and ensure security-by-design for products with digital elements sold in the EU.

infrastructure.name: CRA Single Reporting Platform
infrastructure.description: A centralized platform managed by ENISA through which manufacturers must submit vulnerability and incident notifications as required by the Cyber Resilience Act.
